info.name: test suite
info.description: # 🚀 Get started here This template guides you through CRUD operations (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), variables, and tests. ## 🔖 **How to use this template** #### **Step 1: Send requests** RESTful APIs allow you to perform CRUD operations using the POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE HTTP methods. This collection contains each of these [request](https://learning.postman.com/docs/sending-requests/requests/) types. Open each request and click "Send" to see what happens. #### **Step 2: View responses** Observe the response tab for status code (200 OK), response time, and size. #### **Step 3: Send new Body data** Update or add new data in "Body" in the POST request. Typically, Body data is also used in PUT request. ``` json { "name": "Add your name in the body" } ``` #### **Step 4: Update the variable** Variables enable you to store and reuse values in Postman. We have created a [variable](https://learning.postman.com/docs/sending-requests/variables/) called `base_url` with the sample request [https://postman-api-learner.glitch.me](https://postman-api-learner.glitch.me). Replace it with your API endpoint to customize this collection. #### **Step 5: Add tests in the "Tests" tab** Tests help you confirm that your API is working as expected. You can write test scripts in JavaScript and view the output in the "Test Results" tab. ## 💪 Pro tips - Use folders to group related requests and organize the collection. - Add more [scripts](https://learning.postman.com/docs/writing-scripts/intro-to-scripts/) in "Tests" to verify if the API works as expected and execute workflows. ## 💡Related templates [API testing basics](https://go.postman.co/redirect/workspace?type=personal&collectionTemplateId=e9a37a28-055b-49cd-8c7e-97494a21eb54&sourceTemplateId=ddb19591-3097-41cf-82af-c84273e56719) [API documentation](https://go.postman.co/redirect/workspace?type=personal&collectionTemplateId=e9c28f47-1253-44af-a2f3-20dce4da1f18&sourceTemplateId=ddb19591-3097-41cf-82af-c84273e56719) [Authorization methods](https://go.postman.co/redirect/workspace?type=personal&collectionTemplateId=31a9a6ed-4cdf-4ced-984c-d12c9aec1c27&sourceTemplateId=ddb19591-3097-41cf-82af-c84273e56719)

request.description:
This is a GET request and it is used to "get" data from an endpoint. There is no request body for a GET request, but you can use query parameters to help specify the resource you want data on (e.g., in this request, we have `id=1`).

A successful GET response will have a `200 OK` status, and should include some kind of response body - for example, HTML web content or JSON data.

request.description:
This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve information about a specific resource identified by the provided ID and format. The request should include the ID in the URL path and the format as a query parameter.

The last execution of this request returned a 400 status code with a JSON response containing a "message" key.

request.description:
# Search Title

This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve information about titles based on the provided title part.

**Request Body**

- The request body should be in raw format with the following JSON payload:
    
    ```
    {
      "titlePart": ""
    }
    
     ```
    
    - `titlePart` (string, required): The part of the title to search for.

**Response**

- Status: 200
- Content-Type: application/json
- The response will be a JSON array containing information about the titles matching the search criteria. Each object in the array will have the following properties:
    - `titleID` (string): The ID of the title.
    - `type` (string): The type of the title.
    - `originalTitle` (string): The original title of the title.
    - `titlePoster` (string): The poster of the title.
    - `startYear` (string): The start year of the title.
    - `endYear` (string): The end year of the title.
    - `genres` (array): An array of objects containing genre information with the following properties:
        - `genreTitle` (string): The title of the genre.
    - `titleAkas` (array): An array of objects containing alternative title information with the following properties:
        - `akaTitle` (string): The alternative title.
        - `regionAbbrev` (string): The region abbreviation.
    - `principals` (array): An array of objects containing principal information with the following properties:
        - `nameID` (string): The ID of the name.
        - `name` (string): The name of the principal.
        - `category` (string): The category of the principal.
    - `rating` (object): An object containing rating information with the following properties:
        - `avRating` (string): The average rating.
        - `nVotes` (string): The number of votes.

request.description:
# Search Title

This endpoint is used to search for titles.

## Request

- Method: GET
- URL: `https://localhost:9876/searchtitle`

### Query Parameters
- `titlePart` (string, required): The part of the title to search for.

## Response

- Status: 204
- Content-Type: text/xml

request.description:
# Search Title

This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve information about titles based on the provided title part.

**Request Body**

- The request body should be in raw format with the following JSON payload:
    
    ```
    {
      "titlePart": ""
    }
    
     ```
    
    - `titlePart` (string, required): The part of the title to search for.

**Response**

- Status: 200
- Content-Type: application/json
- The response will be a JSON array containing information about the titles matching the search criteria. Each object in the array will have the following properties:
    - `titleID` (string): The ID of the title.
    - `type` (string): The type of the title.
    - `originalTitle` (string): The original title of the title.
    - `titlePoster` (string): The poster of the title.
    - `startYear` (string): The start year of the title.
    - `endYear` (string): The end year of the title.
    - `genres` (array): An array of objects containing genre information with the following properties:
        - `genreTitle` (string): The title of the genre.
    - `titleAkas` (array): An array of objects containing alternative title information with the following properties:
        - `akaTitle` (string): The alternative title.
        - `regionAbbrev` (string): The region abbreviation.
    - `principals` (array): An array of objects containing principal information with the following properties:
        - `nameID` (string): The ID of the name.
        - `name` (string): The name of the principal.
        - `category` (string): The category of the principal.
    - `rating` (object): An object containing rating information with the following properties:
        - `avRating` (string): The average rating.
        - `nVotes` (string): The number of votes.

## Request

- Method: GET
- URL: `https://localhost:9876/searchtitle`

### Query Parameters
- `titlePart` (string, required): The part of the title to search for.

## Response

- Status: 204
- Content-Type: text/xml
